A brand audit prepared in advance of the Chicago portfolio review. The final of six AWC operating companies in this series.
Page 01
02 · State of the Brand Today

The brand the rest of the portfolio inherits from. Itself underleveraged.

Page 02

Leopardo Construction is the foundation of Alexander West Capital. Founded in 1977, the firm built the commercial construction business that became the operating credibility behind every other entity in the portfolio. Enlyt, Laystrom, DFH Connect, Grand Kahn Electric, and GATOR all benefit, directly or indirectly, from the trust Leopardo Construction earned over four decades of building.

The audit is not about damage repair. Leopardo Construction is the strongest brand in the portfolio — on every relative measure. The audit is about asking a different question: is the brand carrying as much weight as it could, for the portfolio as a whole, given what it has earned?

What works.

Operating Credibility
Nearly five decades of commercial construction work. Real projects. Real relationships. Real continuity through multiple economic cycles. This is the asset every other portfolio company benefits from.
Family Identity
The Leopardo name carries weight in Chicago commercial construction circles. A name with that kind of recognition is the most efficient marketing budget a company can have. Most competitors spend millions per year trying to build what Leopardo Construction has by default.
Strategic Position
Anchor of an integrated portfolio that spans construction, electrical, hardware, smart systems, software, and capital. No competitor in the Chicago market has assembled this. The position is unique and largely undefended.

What could work harder.

Portfolio Endorsement
Leopardo Construction’s brand currently does not visibly endorse the rest of the portfolio. GATOR carries the line “A Leopardo Technology.” The other four companies should inherit similar lineage signaling.
Generational Story
The founding, the family continuity, and the next generation of leadership are the most defensible story in the portfolio. They show up in conversation but not consistently in published material. The next generation of buyers does not yet know what the current generation knows.
Portfolio Strategy
The decision to build an integrated portfolio — construction plus electrical plus hardware plus software plus capital — is a sophisticated capital strategy. Publicly, it reads as a collection of unrelated companies. The strategic logic is largely invisible to the market.
03 · The Anchor

What the rest of the portfolio inherits from Leopardo Construction.

Page 03

Leopardo Construction is the source of trust the other five companies draw from. Each one has its own product, its own audience, and its own competitive context — but each one is more credible because Leopardo Construction stands behind it.

Grand Kahn Electric
Trusted in the Chicago commercial construction supply chain that Leopardo built relationships across.
Enlyt
Sells smart-building tech to developers who already work with Leopardo on the structures themselves.
Laystrom
Supplies hardware to general contractors who learned the Laystrom name through Leopardo specifications.
DFH Connect
Sells software to door and hardware distributors. AWC Technology built it from inside the trade Leopardo serves.
GATOR
Carries the explicit endorsement line: A Leopardo Technology. Inherits municipal credibility from Leopardo Efficiency.

The asymmetry is the opportunity. Leopardo Construction quietly enables the trust of five other companies but is not paid for it in market visibility. A portfolio-level brand system makes that inheritance structural and visible — without diluting Leopardo Construction’s standalone identity.

04 · Historical Wins

The project portfolio that defines the brand.

Page 04

Decades of major commercial work. The specific projects, named clients, signature buildings, and notable installs that anchor Leopardo Construction’s reputation are best surfaced through structured intake with the senior team.

Vertical Depth

Commercial Build Categories

Across Chicagoland and beyond

Leopardo Construction’s project portfolio spans the major commercial verticals: corporate office, hospitality, healthcare, education, industrial, and retail. The breadth is itself a defensibility — few firms in the market can speak credibly to all of these. Each vertical represents a separate set of buyer relationships and project archetypes worth surfacing individually.

Generational Continuity

Founding through Today

Family business since 1977

Construction firms that survive multiple economic cycles are rare. Construction firms that survive while remaining family-owned and operated are rarer. Leopardo Construction’s continuity is a credibility moat that compounds annually and cannot be replicated by newer competitors regardless of capital.

Trade Network

Subcontractor & Vendor Relationships

The operating ecosystem

Leopardo Construction has built relationships with hundreds of subcontractors, vendors, architects, and trade partners across decades of work. This operating network is what allows the portfolio strategy to function — Enlyt, Laystrom, and the others operate inside a trade network Leopardo has already cultivated. The network itself is brand equity.

The first ninety days of the engagement should include structured interviews with senior Leopardo Construction leadership to surface the specific projects, named clients, signature buildings, and operating numbers that should anchor public-facing brand material. The depth of the portfolio is institutional knowledge today. The audit’s job is to make it legible to the next decade of buyers.
05 · The Brand Gap

The strongest brand in the portfolio, undermarketed at the portfolio level.

Page 05

Leopardo Construction is the most defensible brand in the family. It is also the most underleveraged.

The other five audits in this series identified gaps between what each company has earned and what it presents. Leopardo Construction’s gap is structural and different. The brand is strong. The standalone identity is intact. The reputation in the Chicago commercial construction market is real. The gap is at the portfolio level: Leopardo Construction’s decades of credibility currently transfer to the other five companies through informal relationships rather than through visible brand architecture. That transfer can be made structural — and when it is, the entire portfolio appreciates.

What the brand has earned
Nearly five decades of major commercial work. A family name with weight in Chicago. Deep trade relationships across architects, GCs, subs, and developers. Multi-cycle survival as evidence of operating discipline.
What the brand does today
Maintains its own commercial construction operation at a high standard. Wins work through reputation, referral, and continuity. Operates inside its own brand silo without consistently endorsing the rest of the portfolio.
What a portfolio-level brand does
Makes Leopardo Construction’s credibility visible across every portfolio touchpoint. Enlyt, Laystrom, DFH Connect, Grand Kahn, and GATOR all inherit identifiable lineage signaling. The market sees an integrated commercial infrastructure family, not a collection of unrelated companies.
What this is worth
Higher pricing power across the portfolio. Stronger Series A valuation for GATOR. Better LP-facing narrative for the broader family office. Reduced customer acquisition cost for every entity. The single highest-leverage brand investment in the family.
06 · Recommendations

Six moves over the first 90 days.

Page 06

The Leopardo Construction audit is the audit where the recommendations are not just about Leopardo Construction. They are about how the anchor brand serves the rest of the portfolio.

01

Conduct the senior team intake.

Structured interviews with Leopardo Construction leadership to surface the projects, clients, signature buildings, operating numbers, and family stories that should anchor public-facing brand material. The same brand archaeology recommended for Laystrom — calibrated to a firm with more visible history to work with.

02

Activate the founding story.

A real About page anchored in the 1977 founding, the family ownership, the generational leadership transition, and the strategic decision to expand into an integrated portfolio. The current site should clearly state what the family has built and where it is going. This becomes the anchor story for the broader portfolio narrative.

03

Produce signature case study films.

Three to five cinematic project films, each 90-120 seconds, telling the story of a Leopardo Construction signature build. Real footage, real clients, real testimony. Distributed across the website, the AWC family office materials, LinkedIn, and direct outreach to developers. These films also serve as the visual standard the rest of the portfolio aspires to.

04

Build the portfolio endorsement system.

Define the lineage signaling structure that lets every other portfolio company carry visible Leopardo connection — without compromising their own standalone identity. GATOR’s “A Leopardo Technology” line is the template. Enlyt, Laystrom, DFH Connect, and Grand Kahn each get an analogous lineage line. The portfolio becomes recognizable across surfaces.

05

Introduce the family voice.

Senior leadership becomes publicly visible — LinkedIn presence, occasional industry talks, podcast appearances, signed bylines. The family is the brand. Buyers in commercial construction at this tier choose firms on relationships with principals, not on marketing volume. The principals should be findable.

06

Anchor the AWC portfolio identity system.

Leopardo Construction’s typography, color, voice, and design language become the anchor for the broader portfolio system. Each other company gets a distinct accent within the family palette. The visual coherence makes the strategic logic visible: this is one integrated commercial infrastructure family, not five separate ventures.

07 · Proposed Positioning
Building Chicago since 1977. And the businesses that build with us.
A line that names the founding, the work, and the integrated portfolio in one breath. Positions Leopardo Construction not as a single firm but as the anchor of a family of commercial infrastructure operating companies. Works on the website, on a developer-facing proposal, and at the top of every piece of AWC family office material.
Brand Audit · Leopardo Construction
Prepared by Brandon Cole · VDO Aigency · May 2026
